title: Quadratic formula
description: Solve quadratic equations using the quadratic formula. For example, solve -9x+10x²+8=14.
topic_path: Module 4: Polynomial and quadratic expressions, equations, and functions
Topic B: Lessons 14-15: The quadratic formula

hints:
Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
3x- 9x^2+10 &= 0
\\\\
-9x^{2}+3x+10 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -9, b = 3, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
6- 6x^2- 3x &= 0
\\\\
-6x^{2}-3x+6 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -6, b = -3, c = 6$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
3x - 9x^2 &= -10
\\\\
3x - 9x^2 + 10 &= 0
\\\\
-9x^{2}+3x+10 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -9, b = 3, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
6 - 6x^2 &= 3x
\\\\
6 - 6x^2 - 3x &= 0
\\\\
-6x^{2}-3x+6 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -6, b = -3, c = 6$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
3x &= 9x^2 - 10
\\\\
3x - 9x^2 + 10 &=0
\\\\
-9x^{2}+3x+10 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -9, b = 3, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
10 &= -4x + 3x^2
\\\\
10 + 4x &= 3x^2
\\\\
10 + 4x - 3x^2 &= 0
\\\\
-3x^{2}+4x+10 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -3, b = 4, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
-6x &= 3x^2 + 1
\\\\
-6x - 3x^2 &= 1
\\\\
-6x - 3x^2 - 1 &= 0
\\\\
-3x^{2}-6x-1 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -3, b = -6, c = -1$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
6 + 2x^2 - 3x &= 8x^2
\\\\
6 - 6x^2 - 3x &= 0
\\\\
-6x^{2}-3x+6 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -6, b = -3, c = 6$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
10 - 9x^2 + 4x &= -6x^2
\\\\
10 - 3x^2 + 4x &= 0
\\\\
-3x^{2}+4x+10 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -3, b = 4, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
-6x - 1 + 5x^2 &= 8x^2
\\\\
-6x - 1 - 3x^2 &= 0
\\\\
-3x^{2}-6x-1 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -3, b = -6, c = -1$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
10 - 6x - 9x^2 &= -9x
\\\\
10 + 3x - 9x^2 &= 0
\\\\
-9x^{2}+3x+10 &= 0
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -9, b = 3, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}
-6x^2 + 6 - 2x &= x
\\\\
-6x^2 + 6 - 3x &= 0
\\\\
-6x^{2}-3x+6 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -6, b = -3, c = 6$

$\begin{align}

\\\\
4x + 10 - 3x^2 &= 0
\\\\
-3x^{2}+4x+10 &= 0 
\end{align}$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -3, b = 4, c = 10$

$\begin{align}

\\\\
10 + 3x - 9x^2 &= 0
\\\\
-9x^{2}+3x+10 &= 0

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -9, b = 3, c = 10$

Get the equation into the form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$

In our case, $a = -6, b = -3, c = 6$

$\begin{align}
-5x^2 + 7x &= -9

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

In our case, $a = -5, b = 7, c = 9$

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

$\begin{align}

\\\\
10x^2 - 6 - 9x &= 0
\\\\

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:

Use the quadratic formula to solve $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$:
